title: Manilkara excelsa
intro: Manilkara excelsa, also known as the balata tree, is a species of tropical tree native to the Caribbean and Central America, characterized by its large, evergreen leaves and edible fruits. The health benefits of Manilkara excelsa include its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to protect against various diseases. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, such as balatine and balatinine, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Manilkara excelsa include teas, extracts, and infusions, which are traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems.
taxonomy: Manilkara excelsa is classified in the family Sapotaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the sapodilla family. This family includes over 200 species of trees and shrubs native to tropical and subtropical regions around the world. The genus Manilkara is a group of trees that are native to tropical regions of the Americas and Africa. Manilkara excelsa is a species within this genus that is native to tropical regions of Central and South America. Manilkara excelsa is also known as the boxwood tree or the boxwood sapote.
